metadata.description: The declarative NetworkManager desired-state for a host interface — addressing method, routes, DNS, and bond/bridge/VLAN membership — as a first-class estate record (ADR-023). The body conforms to the NMstate interface schema by reference (Tier-2 adopt): UDLM owns identity + the version-pinned conformance pointer, NMstate owns the config body. Realized onto the host by a NetworkManager provider (e.g. Ansible, Kubernetes-NMState); maps 1:1 to OpenShift NodeNetworkConfigurationPolicy.spec.desiredState. Supersedes bespoke per-tool host-network variables.

context.purpose: Captures a host interface's desired network configuration — addressing, routes, DNS, bond/bridge/VLAN membership — as declarative state a provider applies.
context.plain_description: What a host interface's network configuration should be, in NMstate's own schema, carried as an opaque body against a pinned schema version — the profile does not re-describe NMstate's fields. A NetworkManager-family provider applies it (Ansible, Kubernetes-NMState); the state read back from the host is published, and a difference between desired and discovered is drift. It replaces per-tool host-network variable files with one governed record per interface.
use_when: You need host interface config (static addressing, routes, VLANs on a bond) declared once and converged by automation.
You need drift in host networking detected from data, not by logging into hosts.
not_for: The interface device itself — Hardware.NetworkInterface; the profile configures a device that type inventories.
BIOS settings — Hardware.BiosProfile: the same opaque-body-plus-pinned-registry pattern, for firmware.
works_with: Hardware.NetworkInterface — the adapter or port the profile applies to.
Network.VLAN — the segments the configured VLANs and sub-interfaces ride.

lifecycle.decommission: NetworkManager reverts/removes the connection; Discovered reflects absent. Non-destructive to data; may drop host connectivity, so ordering is host-network-sensitive.

nmstate_schema_version.description: The pinned NMstate interface-schema version the `desired_state` body conforms to (the conformance pointer; NMstate releasing a new version does NOT force a UDLM schema change — the Tier-2 litmus).

desired_state.description: Opaque NMstate desired-state body — `state`, `ipv4`/`ipv6` (`enabled`/`dhcp`/`autoconf`/`address[]`), the type sub-objects (`vlan`/`link-aggregation`/`bridge`/`mac-vlan`), `routes.config[]`, `dns-resolver.config`. NOT restated field-by-field (adopt-by-reference); interpreted against `nmstate_schema_version`. 1:1 with NNCP.spec.desiredState.

applied.description: Whether the desired state is applied and matches on the host (Realized).

discovered_state.description: The NMstate current-state read back from the host; a diff vs desired_state is drift (OBS-001).

relationships.name: configures
relationships.description: configures — the adapter/port this profile applies to. Host-side declaration (dependent-side rule).

adopts.role: the whole config body by reference (Tier-2): state/ipv4/ipv6/vlan/link-aggregation/bridge/mac-vlan/routes/dns-resolver — NetworkManager declarative desired-state, 1:1 with OpenShift NNCP
